title: Podman release for armhf architecture
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Currently, there is no armhf build for Podman, which eliminates the use of Podman for operating systems such as Raspbian. Although you can probably compile it with the source code, it would be nice to have a pre-compiled binary to use. This prevents the user from installing all the needed dependencies in order to compile Podman by himself.

Installing with Snap gives the following error:

> pi@raspberrypi:~ $ sudo snap install podman
> error: snap "podman" is not available on this architecture (armhf) but exists on other
>        architectures (amd64).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. on armhf architecture: sudo snap install podman

**Describe the results you received:**
> pi@raspberrypi:~ $ sudo snap install podman
> error: snap "podman" is not available on this architecture (armhf) but exists on other
>        architectures (amd64).

**Describe the results you expected:**
Having a compiled binary for armhf available.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Tested on a Raspberry Pi 3 Model B with Raspbian Buster. Latest image (as of the time of writing) with snapd installed.

comments.body: Any chance you would be willing to open a PR to make this happen?

> Any chance you would be willing to open a PR to make this happen?

Thanks for your reply. If I could, I would. However, I'm unexperienced in this field. If someone could guide me through the process or has some handy resources on the internet I could use, I would be more than happy to give it a try.
my understanding is raspberrian was based on debian?

The cross-distribution packaging is now happening on the Open Build Service. https://build.opensuse.org/project/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable provides packages for ARM. Maybe they'll do the job?
If you prefer building your own distribution, the OpenEmbedded layer meta-virtualization has recipe for podman (https://git.yoctoproject.org/cgit/cgit.cgi/meta-virtualization/).
@agners Could you open a PR to add that link to the install.md file?
@vrothberg care to knock this out for bug week?
@vrothberg Indeed, the openSUSE page has builds for armHF (more specifically Raspbian). This basically solves the issue. Thanks alot!

Installation instructions on podman wiki show invalid dependency graph:

```
The following packages have unmet dependencies:
podman:armhf : Depends: containers-common:armhf (>= 1.1.1~2) but it is not installable
Recommends: crun:armhf but it is not going to be installed
Recommends: slirp4netns:armhf but it is not going to be installed
Recommends: uidmap:armhf but it is not going to be installed
Recommends: varlink:armhf but it is not going to be installed
E: Unable to correct problems, you have held broken packages.
```
